heroHeadline: El Mayo Sentencing 4 Days Away — 623 Days Since Capture, 577 Days of Cartel Civil War
heroSubtitle: 623 days since Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada's alleged kidnapping — 2,412+ homicides, 9,000+ families displaced. El Mayo faces mandatory life imprisonment and a $15 billion forfeiture at his April 13 sentencing before Judge Brian Cogan (EDNY). CJNG named Juan Carlos Valencia González as new leader (El País, Apr 6), placing the Chapitos-CJNG alliance under uncertainty: hundreds of CJNG fighters were deployed to Sinaloa supporting Los Chapitos but the new leadership's commitment to this expensive deployment is unconfirmed. La Mayiza controls approximately 90% of former Chapitos territory. White House ONDCP (Apr 2) honored first-ever Sinaloa Cartel narcoterrorism convictions under the FTO designation, including 146 arrests in an East African distribution network bust (900K fentanyl pills). Good Friday (Apr 4): 8 murdered in Culiacán — 34th municipal police officer killed since war began. Escuinapa surge: 4 officers killed (Mar 31), army clash with cartel-recruited minors (Apr 5). Mexican Navy dismantled Chapitos-linked meth lab in Cosalá (Apr 3, 1,000L of P2P).
footerNote: Data sourced from Noroeste, Riodoce, Proceso, InSight Crime, Reuters, AP, Animal Político, ACLED, DEA, SEDENA, SEMAR, Al Jazeera, AFP, Infobae, La Jornada, CBC News, Excelsior, El Diario del Noroeste, White House ONDCP, DOJ
